subject: Chemistry
q: In the Group analysis of cations, $\mathrm{Ba}^{2+} ~\&~ \mathrm{Ca}^{2+}$ are precipitated respectively as
question: In the Group analysis of cations, $\mathrm{Ba}^{2+} ~\&~ \mathrm{Ca}^{2+}$ are precipitated respectively as
solution: In Group V of cation analysis, both Ba²⁺ and Ca²⁺ are precipitated by adding Na₂CO₃ or (NH₄)₂CO₃ solution. Ba²⁺ precipitates as BaCO₃ (white precipitate) and Ca²⁺ precipitates as CaCO₃ (white precipitate). Both form insoluble carbonates under these conditions, making the answer carbonate & carbonate.

subject: Chemistry
q: In the wet tests for detection of various cations by precipitation, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Ba</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> cations are detected by obtaining precipitate of
question: In the wet tests for detection of various cations by precipitation, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Ba</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> cations are detected by obtaining precipitate of
solution:
Ba2+ belongs to Vth Group and hence they are precipitated with CO32- to give ppt of BaCO3. In Wet tests for cations, (NH4)2CO3 is used as a group reagent for fifth group cations(Ba2+,Ca2+,Sr2+).

Ba2++(NH4)2CO3→BaCO3+NH4⊕

subject: Chemistry
q: By usual analysis, 1.00 g of compound $(\mathrm{X})$ gave 1.79 g of magnesium pyrophosphate. The percentage of phosphorus in compound $(X)$ is : (nearest integer) (Given, molar mass in $\mathrm{g} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}: \mathrm{O}=16, \mathrm{Mg}=24, \mathrm{P}=31$)
question: By usual analysis, 1.00 g of compound $(\mathrm{X})$ gave 1.79 g of magnesium pyrophosphate. The percentage of phosphorus in compound $(X)$ is : (nearest integer) (Given, molar mass in $\mathrm{g} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}: \mathrm{O}=16, \mathrm{Mg}=24, \mathrm{P}=31$)
solution:
Given: 1.00 g of compound (X) produces 1.79 g of Mg₂P₂O₇.
Molar mass of Mg₂P₂O₇ = 2(24) + 2(31) + 7(16) = 222 g/mol.
Moles of Mg₂P₂O₇ = 1.79 / 222 = 0.00806 mol.
Since each mole of Mg₂P₂O₇ contains 2 moles of P, moles of P = 2 × 0.00806 = 0.01612 mol.
Mass of P = 0.01612 × 31 = 0.4997 g ≈ 0.50 g.
Percentage of phosphorus = (0.50 / 1.00) × 100 = 50%.

subject: Chemistry
q:
The number of white coloured salts among the following is .......

A SrSO4   B MgNH4PO4   C BaCrO4

D Mn(OH)2   E PbSO4   F PbCrO4

G AgBr      H PbI2    I CaC2O4

J Fe(OH)2CH3COO
question:
The number of white coloured salts among the following is .......

A SrSO4   B MgNH4PO4   C BaCrO4

D Mn(OH)2   E PbSO4   F PbCrO4

G AgBr      H PbI2    I CaC2O4

J Fe(OH)2CH3COO
solution:
The colour of the salts are as follows,

SrSO4- white
MgNH4PO4 - white
BaCrO4 - yellow
Mn(OH)2 - white
PbSO4 - white
PbCrO4 - yellow
AgBr - pale yellow
PbI2 - yellow
CaC2O4 - white

Fe(OH)2CH3COO – Brown Red

Hence the number of salts which are white in colour is 5.
